summary: Ran pytest tests/ -n auto 8 times consecutively (5 requested + 3 extra to characterize a new flake). Result: 7/8 runs were 64/64 clean; 1/8 (run 5) failed with 63/64 on a DIFFERENT test than the one main agent was fixing.

log_tail_fix_verification.result: PASSED in ALL 8 runs — main agent's fix (500KB tail window + 12-iteration polling loop) is working correctly. The original log-tail race is resolved.

minor.issue: test_teacher_can_move_student_batch flakes ~12% under -n auto (1/8 runs). AssertionError at line 150: `assert row is not None` — the shared student is missing from the students list after a successful batch move. Root cause is shared-state contention across parallel tests, not a product bug.

rca_of_the_new_flake.failing_assertion: line 150: assert row is not None
rca_of_the_new_flake.reproduction: Under `pytest -n auto` (8-worker xdist), the test intermittently (~1/8) fails to find the moved student in the follow-up GET /courses/{cid}/students at line 148.
rca_of_the_new_flake.root_cause: The test operates on SEEDED SHARED accounts (student_token/teacher_token fixtures from conftest) and picks students[0] from the first teacher course with a batch. Between the successful PUT at line 138-144 and the verification GET at line 148, another parallel test — most likely test_phase9_10_enrollment.py which enrols/moves/unenrols the same shared student on the same shared course, or a portal_mode toggle from line 125/129 racing with another test — mutates that student's enrollment. This is a test-isolation problem, not a product bug (PUT returned 200 with correct batch_id at line 145).
evidence: test_phase9_10_enrollment.py lines 40-72 enrols/moves shared student on shared courses
test_iteration6_bugfix.py line 97/107/137 also PUTs .../students/{id}/batch on shared course
Line 125/129 in the failing test toggles portal_mode globally (demo <-> live), a well-known shared-state hazard that iteration_10 flagged as fixed
Failure is non-deterministic (1/8), typical of a race window between two API calls (line 138 PUT and line 148 GET)
recommended_mitigation: Option A (preferred, hermetic): Rewrite test_teacher_can_move_student_batch to create its own TEST_ course + batch + student via an admin-created TEST_ user, exactly like test_move_batch_invalid_returns_error (line 166 comment: 'Hermetic: create a throwaway TEST_ course so parallel runs don't collide...'). This is the same pattern the file already uses elsewhere.

Option B (cheaper, weaker): After the PUT succeeds, if the student is missing from students2, retry the GET 2-3 times with 500ms backoff before asserting — masks the race but keeps shared-state coupling.
Option C: Remove the portal_mode toggle at lines 125/129 by making the enrol path work under the current portal_mode, or serialize this test with a filelock similar to iteration 10's fix.

action_items: MUST FIX before deployment: Make test_teacher_can_move_student_batch hermetic — create its own TEST_ course/student instead of reusing seeded shared fixtures. Reference the pattern already used in the same file at test_move_batch_invalid_returns_error (line 167 comment).
The log-tail fix from iteration_11 is confirmed working (8/8 for that specific test); no further action needed on the admin-BCC-email test.
critical_code_review_comments: test_iteration5_bugfix.py line 125/129: toggling global portal_mode inside a test is a shared-state hazard under -n auto. Even wrapped in try/finally, another parallel test observing portal_mode during the toggle window will see inconsistent state.

test_iteration5_bugfix.py line 121-134: picking students[0] from a shared course is not isolation-safe. If a parallel test unenrols that student, the subsequent verification GET at line 148 will not find them.

The polling-loop pattern introduced for the admin-BCC email test at lines 232-244 is a good template — consider applying the same eventual-consistency polling to line 148 as a defense-in-depth measure.

success_rate.backend: 87.5% (7/8 full-suite runs clean); 100% (8/8) for the specific log-tail test main agent was fixing

test_credentials: Used seeded credentials from test_credentials context: admin@rgpacademy.com/Admin@123, teacher@rgpacademy.com/Teacher@123, student@rgpacademy.com/Student@123
seed_data_creation: None — used existing seed data only
context_for_next_testing_agent: iteration_11's log-tail fix is fully validated (8/8 passes on the target test). The NEW flake in iteration_12 is a separate shared-state race in test_teacher_can_move_student_batch (line 150) — needs hermetic rewrite before deployment. Do NOT deploy yet.
